label: PagerDuty
description: Connect to PagerDuty to manage incidents, on-call schedules, services, and escalation policies.

form.title: Connect PagerDuty
form.subtitle: Paste your PagerDuty REST API key to give Anton access to incidents and on-call data.

methods.label: REST API Key
methods.description: Paste a PagerDuty REST API key. Takes 30 seconds — no OAuth setup required. Supports both full-access and read-only keys.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a PagerDuty REST API Key

PagerDuty issues two kinds of REST API keys: **User API keys** (scoped to a single user's permissions) and **Account API keys** (scoped to the full account). Either works with Anton.

### Account-level API key (recommended for broad access)

1. Log in to your PagerDuty account.
2. Navigate to **Integrations → API Access Keys** in the top menu.
3. Click **Create New API Key**.
4. Give the key a description like `Anton`.
5. Choose **Full Access** or **Read-only** depending on whether you want Anton to be able to acknowledge/resolve incidents or just read data.
6. Click **Create Key**. Copy the key immediately — it is only shown once.
7. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

### User API key (scoped to your account)

1. Click your avatar in the top-right corner → **My Profile**.
2. Scroll to the **User Settings** tab → **Create API User Token**.
3. Give it a description, copy the token, and paste it below.

### Notes

- Account API keys are only visible to PagerDuty admins and account owners.
- If you lose the key, you must regenerate it — old key cannot be retrieved.
- Read-only keys cannot create, acknowledge, or resolve incidents.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: REST API Key
fields.description: Found under Integrations → API Access Keys. Shown only once at creation time.

fields.name: subdomain
fields.label: PagerDuty Subdomain
fields.description: The subdomain of your PagerDuty account (e.g. 'yourcompany' from yourcompany.pagerduty.com). Optional — Anton will infer it from the API key if left blank.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (App Integration)
methods.description: Authorize Anton via PagerDuty's OAuth 2.0 flow using your own registered PagerDuty app. Requires creating an app in the PagerDuty Developer Mode.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 via PagerDuty App Registration

Use this method if your organization requires OAuth-based access instead of static API keys.

### Prerequisites

- You must have a PagerDuty account with **Developer Mode** enabled (available on Business and Enterprise plans).

### Steps

1. Log in to PagerDuty and go to **Integrations → Developer Tools → My Apps**.
2. Click **Create New App**.
3. Fill in a name (e.g. `Anton`) and description. Set **Category** to anything appropriate.
4. Under **Functionality**, enable **OAuth 2.0** and select **Authorization Code Grant**.
5. Add `http://localhost` as a **Redirect URL** (Anton uses a local callback server).
6. Click **Save**. PagerDuty shows your **Client ID** and **Client Secret** — copy both.
7. Paste the Client ID and Client Secret into the fields below, then click **Submit**.
8. A browser window will open for you to authorize the app with your PagerDuty credentials.

### Notes

- Select `read` and `write` scopes to allow Anton to manage incidents; `read` alone is sufficient for read-only use.
- OAuth tokens can be revoked from **My Profile → Connected Applications** in PagerDuty.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From your PagerDuty app under Integrations → Developer Tools → My Apps.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID from the same PagerDuty app registration page.
